question: Will SpaceX's next attempt to land a Falcon 9 rocket on a barge be successful?
resolution_criteria:
SpaceX's Falcon 9 rockets are designed to be re-used after landing vertically on a floating barge-based landing site. SpaceX has successfully (and might we add awesomely) landed a rocket on land, but so far has not succeeded with a barge.
Will SpaceX's next attempt to land a Falcon 9 on a barge be a success?
Resolution is positive if the Falcon 9 rocket sustains little enough damage that it can be re-used, and negative if the rocket is in either small or large pieces on or near the landing site.
(Note: the closing and resolution time of this question will be retroactively changed to the launch time of the next SpaceX Falcon 9 launch that attempts a barge landing.)
